questions.question: Producer-Consumer problem
key_points: Synchronization between multiple threads accessing shared resources
Handles buffer overflow and underflow scenarios
Essential for task queues and message brokers

questions.question: Designing high-performance multithreading
key_points: Use atomic variables for lock-free operations
Use appropriate thread pool sizes (CPU-bound: ~cores, IO-bound: ~2*cores)

questions.question: Dependency Injection
key_points: Constructor injection is the most recommended approach
Field injection should be avoided in production code

questions.question: Spring Boot Annotations
@Component vs @Service vs @Repository
key_points: @Component is generic, used when role unclear
@Repository marks data access layer with exception translation
Proper annotation makes code intent and architecture clear

questions.question: Constructor Injection vs Field Injection
key_points: Constructor injection is the recommended approach
Easier to unit test without Spring framework
Prevents circular dependency issues at construction time

questions.question: Proxy in Spring
key_points: Check proxy type using getClass().getName() in debugger

questions.question: AOP in Spring
key_points: AOP enables cross-cutting concerns without modifying business code
@Around is most powerful, can control method execution
Spring uses proxies internally for AOP implementation

questions.question: REST vs SOAP
key_points: REST is modern, lightweight, and resource-oriented
SOAP is legacy, heavy, and operation-oriented
REST uses HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
SOAP uses XML envelope and custom protocol
SOAP still used in legacy/regulated systems

questions.question: Spring Boot AutoConfiguration
Spring Security flow
OAuth2
key_points: Authorization Code flow most common and secure
Access tokens limit scope and duration
JWT tokens contain encoded user information
Never expose client secret to frontend

questions.question: Circuit Breaker pattern
key_points: Failure rate threshold triggers state change
Resilience4j is modern Spring Boot implementation

questions.question: Service Discovery (Eureka)
key_points: Services auto-register with Eureka on startup
Feign Client enables declarative service calls

questions.question: API Gateway
key_points: Single entry point for all client requests

questions.question: Feign Client
key_points: Simpler than RestTemplate for inter-service calls

questions.question: Config Server
Kafka architecture
key_points: Topics divided into partitions for parallelism
Partition key ensures message ordering per partition
Consumer groups balance partitions across consumers
Dead letter queues for failed messages

questions.question: Kafka consumer
key_points: Consumer group distributes partitions across consumers
Offset tracks message position in partition
Manual commit ensures message processing guarantee
Dead letter topic for failed messages

questions.question: Event Driven Architecture
key_points: Kafka or RabbitMQ for event distribution
Dead letter queues for failed events

questions.question: Saga Pattern
Distributed Tracing
key_points: Trace ID tracks request across services
Correlation IDs link logs to traces
